decks.notes: The key concept of this UWx Control deck is to establish control of the game through a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and card advantage engines. By using cards like Force of Will, Swords to Plowshares, and Teferi, Time Raveler, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while setting up its own win conditions. The inclusion of powerful threats like Geist of Saint Traft and Murktide Regent provides a clock to close out the game once control has been established. The deck also utilizes cantrips like Ponder and Brainstorm to ensure consistency and find key pieces when needed.

The deck focuses on utilizing artifacts and graveyard recursion to generate value and create synergies with cards like Goblin Engineer and Emry, Lurker of the Loch. By combining cards like Mox Opal and Lotus Petal with powerful artifact payoffs like Tezzeret, Cruel Captain and Phyrexian Dragon Engine, the deck aims to set up powerful combos and outvalue opponents. Additionally, there are cards like Loki, God of Mischief to disrupt opponents' strategies and ensure the deck can execute its game plan effectively. The lands and artifacts in the deck support its synergy and combo potential.

The main concept of this Mardu Aggro deck is to quickly and aggressively put pressure on the opponent through a combination of efficient creatures like Guide of Souls and Voice of Victory, removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and Cabal Therapy, and disruptive elements like Thoughtseize and Static Prison. The deck aims to control the board while dealing direct damage with cards like Goblin Bombardment and Orcish Bowmasters, ultimately overwhelming the opponent before they can stabilize. The mana base is carefully constructed to support the aggressive game plan and ensure consistent casting of spells.

The main idea of this deck is to quickly fill the graveyard with powerful creatures like Griselbrand and Archon of Cruelty, and then use cards like Animate Dead and Reanimate to bring them back onto the battlefield for a big advantage. The deck also includes disruptive cards like Unmask and Cabal Therapy to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The inclusion of cards like Atraxa, Grand Unifier and Raph & Mikey, Troublemakers provide additional utility and support for the combo strategy. Ultimately, the goal is to overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures and win the game quickly.

This deck aims to quickly assemble a powerful storm combo by generating mana with cards like Dark Ritual and Lion's Eye Diamond, digging for key pieces with Gamble and Burning Wish, and then casting a flurry of spells to generate a lethal Tendrils of Agony or activate a game-winning Burning Wish. The deck utilizes cards like Echo of Eons and Gaea's Will to refresh resources and continue casting spells, while also featuring disruption in the form of Hexing Squelcher and Runehorn Hellkite to control the opponent's actions. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with a high storm count and win the game in a single explosive turn.

This deck focuses on utilizing aggressive creatures like Thalia, Guardian of Thraben and Badgermole Cub to quickly pressure the opponent. It also includes disruptive elements like Gaddock Teeg and Collector Ouphe to hinder the opponent's game plan. The deck aims to ramp up mana quickly with cards like Noble Hierarch and Knight of the Reliquary, while utilizing cards like Wasteland and Swords to Plowshares for removal and disruption. Overall, this deck aims to quickly establish board presence and disrupt the opponent's strategy to secure a swift victory.

The deck revolves around using Show and Tell to cheat powerful cards like Omniscience and Emrakul, the Aeons Torn into play for free. It also includes multiple ways to protect the combo, such as Force of Will and Veil of Summer, while also including cards like Intuition and Cunning Wish to search for key pieces. The deck aims to quickly assemble its combo pieces to overwhelm the opponent and win the game in a single explosive turn. It also includes some disruption and card draw to help find the combo pieces more consistently.

The main concept of this deck is to apply pressure on the opponent with aggressive creatures like Delver of Secrets, Dragon's Rage Channeler, and Murktide Regent while disrupting their game plan with counterspells like Force of Will and Daze. The deck also includes card filtering and draw spells like Brainstorm and Ponder to ensure a consistent flow of threats. Additionally, the mana denial strategy is implemented through Wasteland to hinder the opponent's ability to cast spells, allowing the deck to maintain tempo and control of the game.

Utilizing a combination of fast mana acceleration and powerful spells, this deck aims to quickly assemble the necessary resources to cast Necrodominance and other key combo pieces, allowing for huge burst damage with Tendrils of Agony or overwhelming board presence with creatures like Chancellor of the Annex. By leveraging efficient mana sources like Lotus Petal and Ritual spells, the deck can execute its game plan at lightning speed, putting pressure on the opponent and ending the game before they have a chance to respond.

This Mardu Aggro deck focuses on creating a fast and aggressive board presence using efficient creatures like Ocelot Pride, Amped Raptor, and Orcish Bowmasters. It also includes disruptive elements like Thoughtseize and Wasteland to control the opponent's game plan. The deck can close out games quickly with Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Goblin Bombardment, while providing some protection with Karakas. Overall, the key concept of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of aggressive creatures and disruptive spells to secure victory.

This deck focuses on using a combination of powerful creatures and disruptive spells to apply early pressure on the opponent. By utilizing cards like Thoughtseize and Swords to Plowshares to disrupt the opponent's strategy, the deck aims to quickly establish a board presence with creatures like Wight of the Reliquary and Scythecat Cub. Green Sun's Zenith enables tutoring for key creatures like Gaddock Teeg or Collector Ouphe to further disrupt the opponent's plans. Grist, the Hunger Tide can provide additional value by creating tokens, while Gaea's Cradle helps to ramp up mana production for explosive plays.

The main concept of this deck is to generate mana quickly using rituals and mana rocks like Mox Opal and Lotus Petal, while protecting your combo pieces with Defense Grid. The goal is to build up storm count and cast Tendrils of Agony for a lethal finish. With access to cards like Burning Wish and Beseech the Mirror, you have versatile options to find the cards you need to execute your combo. The inclusion of cards like Echo of Eons and Wheel of Fate also provide additional ways to dig for key pieces to ensure a successful storm turn.

The deck focuses on quickly filling the graveyard with cards like Buried Alive and Flow State to bring back powerful creatures like Arclight Phoenix. The deck also utilizes spells like Thoughtseize and Force of Will to disrupt the opponent's strategy while using cards like Brainstorm and Ponder to dig for key pieces. The mana base consists of mainly blue and black sources with some red for the Phoenix. The deck aims to deploy threats early and maintain pressure to close out the game before the opponent can stabilize.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game while ramping up mana using Cloudpost / Tron lands. It utilizes powerful counterspells like Force of Will and Force of Negation, along with removal cards like Sink into Stupor and Eldrazi Confluence. The deck aims to quickly assemble the Tron lands to cast big threats like Ugin, the Spirit Dragon and Karn, the Great Creator. The inclusion of cards like Stock Up and The One Ring allow for additional card advantage and flexibility in different game situations.

The key concept of this deck is to disrupt opponent's strategy while maintaining tempo advantage through efficient removal and counter spells. By using cards like Thoughtseize and Force of Will to disrupt opponent's game plan, along with cards like Fatal Push and Nethergoyf to control the board, the deck aims to maintain pressure on the opponent and close out the game quickly. By utilizing powerful card advantage engines like Brainstorm and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student, the deck can out-value opponents while keeping the opponent on the back foot.

This deck focuses on early aggression and disruption while maintaining tempo through card advantage and efficient removal spells. Using cards like Dragon's Rage Channeler and Murktide Regent to pressure opponents, backed up by countermagic like Force of Will and Daze to prevent opposing threats. The deck also utilizes land disruption with Wasteland to slow down opponent's mana development. Overall, the deck aims to control the game pace with a proactive playstyle, utilizing the UR colors for a diverse array of threats and answers.
